scope: Wire the canonical medium MiroFish checkpoint into the existing in-repo watchtower dashboard surface

decision_context.prior_state: The repo had a canonical medium checkpoint and a markdown export, but no downstream in-repo dashboard surface consumed it yet. The legacy viz path is outside the mutable-target contract for this session.
decision_context.decision: Integrate the checkpoint into the watchtower observatory by adding a first-class MiroFish Portfolio page that prefers the saved markdown export and falls back to the saved JSON readout.
decision_context.why_now: The next useful step after the export tranche is to make the current checkpoint visible in an existing dashboard-like surface inside the allowed edit scope.

files_expected.purpose: Adds the MiroFish Portfolio watchtower page and links to it from related observatory pages.
Records why the watchtower surface was used as the in-repo dashboard path.
Captures why the dashboard-surface tranche was added.

planned_steps: Add a MiroFish Portfolio page to the watchtower output
Make that page consume the canonical markdown export when available
Add a JSON-readout fallback and a no-artifact stub
Verify the page through focused tests and a direct runtime generation check

commands: $env:PYTHONPATH='src'; python -c "from chip_labs.watchtower import generate_watchtower_pages; pages=generate_watchtower_pages({}, chip_search_dir='.'); page=next(p for p in pages if p['path']=='MiroFish Portfolio.md'); print(page['content'][:1200])"
expected_checks: The watchtower output includes a new MiroFish Portfolio page
The page links back into the rest of the observatory surface
The generated page resolves to the canonical medium export artifact when that artifact exists

guardrails: Do not touch the legacy viz path in this tranche
Do not re-rank domains inside the watchtower surface
Treat the watchtower page as a surface over the canonical checkpoint, not as a new source of truth
